rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are valid and visible in the current scope before using them in AGREE contracts.
When AGREE reports type mismatches, align the declared types of helpers and constants with the actual types of their expressions.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, only reference identifiers that are explicitly declared or visible in the current component scope.
Validate that all expressions in guarantees and equations have consistent and resolvable types before generating AGREE code.
Avoid introducing package-qualified names or types that are not present in the base AADL model.
When diagnostics report unresolved references, trace back to the root cause and correct or remove the invalid clause.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use only `int`, `real`, and `bool` as scalar types in AGREE constant and equation declarations.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements or references that are not already defined in the AADL model.
Do not assume that identifiers or types are valid without verifying their resolution in the AADL context.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE annex placement in component types vs. implementations
Component type contracts and implementation-level bindings
